summary: Large tournament/menu decomp pass for src/melee/gm/gm_18A5, with supporting header and TmData layout updates. The PR replaced many stubs/untyped byte accesses with C implementations and structured data for tournament bracket drawing, bracket seeding/positioning, menu option handling, player HUD/cursor visibility, and related tournament flows. decomp.dev reported GALE01 matched code rising to 57.13% (+0.20%, +7748 bytes), with 18 new matches and 46 unmatched-item improvements. Human review context is minimal: the PR body only says "Tracking PR as usual" and there were no review comments.
what_changed: Implemented a large set of gm_18A5 tournament/menu functions, replacing many `/// #fn_...` placeholders or partial implementations with C.
Added tournament bracket data structures in gm_18A5.static.h, especially `BracketSrcEntry` and a full 0xDC-byte `BracketEntry` layout with offset-named fields and `GXColor` at x20.
Converted `lbl_80473AB8` from a small anonymous padded struct array to `static BracketEntry lbl_80473AB8[0x40]`, enabling typed access to bracket entries instead of repeated raw byte offsets.

Added/used bracket drawing routines such as `fn_8018C8D4` and `fn_8018D50C`, with explicit `DrawRectangle` calls, local `GXColor` temporaries, and static yellow color labels `lbl_804DA67C` and `lbl_804DA684`.

Implemented bracket setup/positioning logic such as `fn_8018A514` and `fn_8018AA74`, including source-table copying, count-region selection, per-slot coordinate calculation, and special cases based on `TmData->x2E`.
Added a typed `lbl_803DA0D0_t` static-data struct with `icon_model_map`, `rank_thresholds`, and `bounce_y`, plus a `STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(...) == 0x184)`.

Updated gm_18A5.h prototypes from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` or inaccurate signatures to concrete signatures for many functions, including `fn_801913BC(HSD_GObj*)`, `fn_80192938(void)`, `fn_80192BB0(void)`, `fn_80192E6C(void)`, `fn_801967E0(s32)`, and several `HSD_GObj*` callbacks.

Updated `TmData` in types.h: changed `_x1C` padding to `s32 x1C`, packed `TmUnkMenuData`, added `xE` and `xF`, expanded `x37` from 16 to 64 entries, and adjusted padding to preserve the x4B8 selection block.

Adjusted `gmtou.c` to align with the revised `TmData.x37` layout: accesses shifted from fields like `xC/xD/x4/x2/x6` to `xE/xF/x5/x3/x7`, and `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` was replaced with `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj)` in `fn_8019CDBC`.
smart_moves: Promoting `lbl_80473AB8` to a real `BracketEntry[0x40]` made repeated slot fields at offsets x30/x5C/x88/xB4 and status fields x4C/x78/xA4 easier to reason about while preserving offset-based names for matching.
The PR used offset-named fields rather than speculative semantic names for most unknown structures, which is appropriate when the layout is known but final meaning is still uncertain.
Adding `GXStruct.h` and storing colors as `GXColor` avoided treating color components as raw bytes throughout bracket rendering code.

The `TmUnkMenuData` packing and expansion to 64 entries appears coordinated with `gmtou.c` field-offset corrections, preventing one local decomp improvement from corrupting cross-file layout assumptions.
Static assertions on newly typed data (`lbl_803DA0D0_t`) create a reusable guard against accidental layout drift.
The diff kept several `@todo Currently ... match` comments where functions improved but still had register allocation or loop-pattern issues, making future cleanup targets searchable.
decomp_lessons: For large menu/game-mode files, struct reconstruction can unlock many functions at once; here the `BracketEntry` and `TmData.x37` layouts enabled both gm_18A5 and gmtou improvements.
When a table is copied into a runtime buffer, model both the source entry (`BracketSrcEntry`) and runtime entry (`BracketEntry`) instead of forcing one struct to represent both layouts.
Use packed nested structs when the surrounding game data uses byte-dense records; otherwise later array elements and downstream fields can shift silently.
Changing a shared struct such as `TmData` requires auditing sibling files immediately. This PRâ€™s `gmtou.c` changes are evidence that the x37 layout update had external consequences.
For matching-heavy graphics code, preserving the original control-flow shape with many local color temporaries and explicit `DrawRectangle` calls can matter more than writing a compact abstraction.

It is acceptable to leave address-based names and x-offset fields in a high-value matching PR when semantic naming evidence is weak; do not over-name tournament fields before their meanings are proven.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remain address-based, e.g. `fn_8018A514`, `fn_8018AA74`, `fn_8018C8D4`, `fn_8018D50C`, and `fn_80192E6C`.
Game-mode exported functions use the `gm_` prefix when already known, e.g. `gm_80190FE4` and `gm_8019B2DC_OnFrame`.
Frame callbacks retain the `_OnFrame` suffix when applicable.
Unknown global/static labels remain `lbl_80......`, e.g. `lbl_80473AB8`, `lbl_803DA0D0`, `lbl_804DA67C`, and `lbl_804DA684`.
Partially understood struct fields use offset-style names such as `x1C`, `x20`, `x4C`, `x78`, `xA4`, `xE`, and `xF`.
Locally reconstructed structures received descriptive container names where evidence was strong enough: `BracketEntry`, `BracketSrcEntry`, and `lbl_803DA0D0_t`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK`, including in `fn_8018A514` and retained stack padding in `gmtou.c`, to satisfy stack/register-sensitive matching.
Used explicit casts through `u8*` and offset arithmetic for slot records, e.g. `sub = (u8*) entry + slot_idx * 0x2C`, while still exposing the parent entry as `BracketEntry`.
Preserved numerous temporary variables and repeated assignments in drawing functions, especially `GXColor c0...c33`, likely to match compiler register allocation and stack layout.
Used `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` around at least one camera/bracket initialization implementation, indicating targeted compiler-control for difficult matches.
Kept `@todo` comments for known remaining issues such as register allocation fixes and significant rework needs, rather than falsely claiming complete matches.
Switched from macro-style `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` to `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj)` in `gmtou.c`, which coincided with a full match for `fn_8019CDBC` according to the bot report.
Used a `STATIC_ASSERT` on the reconstructed `lbl_803DA0D0_t` data size to lock the expected rodata/data table layout.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review approvals were present in the provided slice.
The PR body only stated: "Tracking PR as usual".
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code at 57.13%, +0.20%, +7748 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 18 new matches and 46 improvements in unmatched items.
Largest reported unmatched-item improvements included `fn_80198EBC` to 97.97%, `fn_8018C8D4` to 79.93%, `fn_8018D50C` to 93.98%, `fn_8019A86C` to 90.91%, and `fn_801953C8` to 87.50%.
follow_up_queries: Inspect later PRs touching `BracketEntry` or `lbl_80473AB8` for semantic field renames beyond x-offset names.
Search for follow-up fixes to `fn_8018C8D4`, `fn_8018D50C`, `fn_80198EBC`, and other functions left below 100% in the bot report.
Audit future changes to `TmData.x37` and `TmUnkMenuData` to confirm the packed 64-entry layout remained valid.
Look for reviews or later commits explaining the `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` versus `GET_JOBJ` choice in `gmtou.c`.
Search for additional tournament-mode PRs by branch or title patterns `gm_18A5`, `gmtou`, and `tournament bracket`.
